mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation measured by CPIAUCSL above 5% year-over-year signals sustained price pressures that force the Federal Reserve to maintain or increase FEDFUNDS, compressing equity valuations through higher discount rates and reducing S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:As of May 2022, CPIAUCSL at 291.474 reflects elevated inflation environment
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL YoY > 5% and FEDFUNDS is rising, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained employment growth measured by PAYEMS expanding month-over-month signals robust labor demand and consumer spending capacity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 151682.0 thousand as of May 2022 reflects strong labor market
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS momentum and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or correlation reverses sign for more than 2 consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts measured by HOUST below 1.5 million annualized units signals weakening demand and construction activity, indicating early-cycle contraction that reduces equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1.5 million annualized units
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1724.0 thousand as of April 2022, near trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of instances when HOUST < 1.5M, or Spearman rank correlation < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production measured by INDPRO declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, which constrains corporate profitability and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as markets reprrice earnings expectations downward.
regime_hint: supply-constrained environment with rising input costs
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 52% of instances following negative INDPRO prints, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment measured by UNRATE above 4.0% combined with declining PAYEMS signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through lower aggregate demand expectations.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.0% while PAYEMS declines month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% as of May 2022, below trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of co-triggered instances (UNRATE > 4.0 AND PAYEMS declining), or joint interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales measured by RSAFS declining month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand and reduced discretionary spending, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 672874.0 million as of May 2022
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or mechanism fails to predict direction in more than 48% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate measured by FEDFUNDS rising above 1.5% signals aggressive monetary tightening that increases borrowing costs and reduces net present value of future corporate cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through multiple compression.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 1.5% and continues rising
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.77% as of May 2022, well below trigger threshold but rising trajectory evident
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle initiated by central bank
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 1.5% and rising, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP measured by GDPC1 growing below 2% annualized combined with declining RSAFS signals late-cycle stagnation where growth momentum is exhausted, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as recession risk premia increase and equity valuations compress.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 2% while RSAFS declines
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19731.119 billion as of January 2022, growth trajectory weakening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 52% of co-triggered instances (GDPC1 growth < 2% AND RSAFS declining), or joint interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 3-year rolling window.
